title: Coleataenia anceps
intro: Coleataenia anceps, also known as False Paspalum, is a type of tropical grass native to Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering health benefits such as wound healing, fever reduction, and relief from gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Coleataenia anceps include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Coleataenia anceps is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is sometimes used in traditional remedies to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Coleataenia anceps is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Panicoideae and the tribe Paniceae. This classification places it within the grass family, which is one of the largest plant families in the world. The genus Coleataenia is a group of grasses native to the Americas. The classification of Coleataenia anceps reflects its evolutionary relationships with other grasses.
